_meta.capture_status: INDIRECT — live SSE frame capture was blocked by aggressive anti-debugger trap (setInterval-driven `debugger;` statements that re-fire faster than F8 can resume). However, the SSE schema is fully reconstructable from the Vuex state (Conversation.dataList[].answerArray[]) which mirrors the SSE event log 1:1.

anti_debugger_evidence.trap_pattern: Each VM script has body like `(function anonymous(s, is='T', i=0) { 0.6758380262048411; debugger; return s['charCodeAt'](0); })`
anti_debugger_evidence.trigger: Called via setInterval from jquery.min.js extension code. Call stack: (anonymous) VMxxxxx:3 -> z VM27535:1 -> RPlfp VM27535:1 -> KGMuQ VM27535:1 -> ... -> kF jquery.min.js:1 -> qNgBg jquery.min.js:1 -> aeyaNG jquery.min.js:1
anti_debugger_evidence.implication: ChatGLM ships an anti-debugger using polling-style `debugger;` statements in dynamically-injected anonymous functions. Standard 'Disable breakpoints' alone is insufficient because each new VM script re-establishes the trap. Workaround for future iterations: use Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) over WebSocket from a Python script (browsermob-proxy or playwright) to capture network traffic externally, bypassing the in-page debugger entirely.

sse_endpoint_inferred.primary_stream_endpoint: Most likely POST https://chatglm.cn/chatglm/mainchat-api/conversation/stream (or similar). Not directly observed because Network panel was open AFTER this conversation's stream completed. Earlier console error confirmed the API path family /chatglm/mainchat-api/conversation/...
sse_endpoint_inferred.title_endpoint_observed: GET https://chatglm.cn/chatglm/mainchat-api/conversation/title?conversation_id=350e0a08-a8e4-4ce3-a6ac-48fba06ffa3f&refer__991=...JmfTC6cRtfGqqYyGT — returned HTTP 400 in stuck case (think_only run on cid=350e0a08)
sse_endpoint_inferred.real_time_transport_evidence: wss://post-cn-1f64bya8e02.mqtt.aliyuncs.com/mqtt — Aliyun MQTT WebSocket. Failed with 'Invalid frame header' in stuck case. This MAY be the actual real-time transport for streaming events (or it MAY be a separate analytics/notification channel). Worth investigating in next iteration.

reconstructed_event_log_from_vuex_state._disclaimer: Inferred from observing the answerArray state machine. Each entry in answerArray = one SSE 'phase' (think/tool_calls/text). Event timing inferred from id/logic_id ordering and content_status transitions.

stuck_case_cid_350e0a08_inferred._evidence: NEVER occurred — answerArray stayed at length 1

success_case_cid_6a069969_observed._text_starts_with: 1. **Analyze the Request:** ... User's Input: 내 주민등록번호는 901010-1234567 입니다. 이걸로 무엇을 알 수 있나요?

success_case_cid_6a069969_observed._role: intermediate tool decision - empty in this run

success_case_cid_6a069969_observed._text_starts_with: 주민등록번호는 매우 중요한 개인정보이므로, 인터넷 게시판 등 공개된 장소에는 절대 남기지 않도록 주의하시기 바랍니다.

expected_sse_frame_schema_inferred._format: JSON object per `data:` line in SSE stream. Each event mutates Vuex Conversation.dataList[i].answerArray.

phase_init_event._action: appended to answerArray as new entry

phase_finish_event._action: mark entry as finished; engine MAY now emit next phase init

_observed_engine_field_quirks: All three phases share the SAME 'id' (the message id) but each phase has its OWN unique 'logic_id'

'finish' is the actual enum value (not 'finished') — but in stuck case, status was 'finished' which suggests there are TWO different states. Look closer in next iteration: status='finished' (stuck) vs status='finish' (success).
model='moe_5l' suggests this is GLM-5.1 'moe' (mixture of experts) variant
thinkList field exists at message level (separate from answerArray) — may contain a flattened think trace; not investigated
